channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: National Assembly Backs Tinubu’s Security Reforms and Proposed State Police Framework
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio say the National Assembly go continue to support President Bola Tinubu on security matters and major economic reforms wey government believe fit improve the country. ...

item.title: Addressing Youth Unemployment in Tanzania Through Strengthening the Informal Sector
item.description: Tanzania is currently dealing with a serious and growing youth unemployment challenge as more young people enter the labour market each year. The article highlights that the formal sector, including g...

item.title: Inquiry ordered into railway alignment issues and large-scale deforestation for Khajuraho–Panna rail project in Madhya Pradesh
item.description: Indian Railways has initiated a formal inquiry into the alignment of the proposed Khajuraho–Panna railway line in Madhya Pradesh after concerns were raised that the originally approved route was unsaf...

item.title: Rescue Efforts Underway for Four Climbers Who Fell Near Denali Pass
item.description: Rescue teams are attempting to reach four climbers who recently fell on Mount McKinley, also known as Denali, the highest peak in North America at 20,310 feet. The incident occurred near Denali Pass a...

item.title: IMD Forecasts Possible Delay in Kerala Monsoon Onset Due to Weak Rainfall Conditions
item.description: The southwest monsoon onset over Kerala may be delayed this year despite the India Meteorological Department (IMD) earlier predicting an early arrival around May 26. According to the latest extended r...

item.title: Former defence minister Linda Reynolds presses ahead with AUKUS amid warnings of an 'imaginary' war
item.description: This analysis argues that Linda Reynolds, the former Australian defence minister, frames her advocacy for the AUKUS security pact as if Australia faces an imminent and existential threat. The piece cr...

item.title: CBSE modified OSM tender conditions after two failed bidding rounds before nationwide Class 12 rollout
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) reportedly floated three successive tenders for its On-Screen Marking (OSM) system before successfully selecting a vendor for the nationwide Class 12 bo...

item.title: Nduduzo Makhathini reflects on collaboration, memory and intergenerational South African jazz
item.description: Nduduzo Makhathini has returned to Durban after an intense international tour spanning the United States, the United Kingdom and parts of Europe, where he performed in multiple formats ranging from hi...

item.title: FG proposes 0.5–1% oil revenue allocation to Police Trust Fund, says Akpabio
item.description: The President of the Nigerian Senate, Godswill Akpabio, has disclosed that the Federal Government is considering a policy to strengthen the Nigeria Police Force by allocating between 0.5% and 1% of re...

item.title: Kenneth Law to plead guilty to assisting suicides linked to toxic substance shipments in Canada and the U.K.
item.description: Kenneth Law, a 60-year-old former chef from the Toronto area, is expected to plead guilty in an Ontario court to 14 counts of assisting suicide. The charges are connected to his alleged role in supply...

item.title: Inquiry told senior UK Special Forces officers avoided police investigation into SAS war crimes allegations
item.description: A public inquiry into alleged war crimes committed by the SAS in Afghanistan between 2010 and 2013 has heard that senior UK Special Forces officers chose not to refer serious allegations to the Royal ...

item.title: US restricts drone flights over World Cup stadiums and fan zones for security reasons
item.description: Authorities in the United States have announced a strict ban on the use of drones and other aircraft around stadiums and fan zones during the upcoming FIFA World Cup. The tournament, which kicks off o...

item.title: Marcos acknowledges Japan’s rapid support in responding to Navotas landfill fire
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. expressed his gratitude to the government of Japan for its swift assistance in response to the recent landfill fire in Navotas. The incident, which affected a major wast...

item.title: KPMG Australia CEO resigns amid auditor conduct investigation
item.description: Andrew Yates, the chief executive of KPMG Australia, has reportedly stepped down immediately following concerns over the firm’s handling of allegations regarding misuse of client data. The situation e...

item.title: Rare trunk-brooding red pipefish confirmed in Sydney waters after eggs observed on male specimen
item.description: A rare observation in Sydney’s Gamay (Botany Bay) has confirmed key aspects of the biology of the red pipefish (Notiocampus ruber), an elusive relative of seahorses and seadragons found only in Austra...

item.title: Students for Trump Co-Founder Ryan Fournier Arrested in Washington on Domestic Violence Charges
item.description: Ryan Fournier, the co-founder and chairman of the conservative political organization Students for Trump, was arrested in Washington, D.C., and charged with domestic violence-related offenses followin...

item.title: Markwayne Mullin Proposes Limiting CBP Presence at Certain Airports, Raising Safety and Travel Concerns
item.description: Senator Markwayne Mullin, nominated by President Donald Trump to serve as Homeland Security Secretary, has reiterated his proposal to withdraw Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officers from airport...

item.title: Pune court frames charges swiftly in Nasrapur child murder case; trial to proceed daily in camera
item.description: A court in Bhor taluka, Pune district, has framed charges against the accused in the Nasrapur child murder case within 28 days of the incident, marking a notably swift progression in the legal process...

item.title: Opinion urges accountability amid rising anger over corruption and immigration pressures in South Africa
item.description: An opinion piece by Advocate Rod Solomons discusses rising public anger in South Africa over corruption, unemployment and immigration pressures, warning that these issues are increasingly being confla...

item.title: Some U.S. cities pause or cover license plate cameras amid concerns over ICE data sharing
item.description: Several U.S. cities that contract with surveillance company Flock Safety are reportedly taking unusual steps to limit the use of their license plate reader camera networks amid growing concerns about ...
